title: sambucus adnata var. puberula
intro: Sambucus adnata var. puberula, also known as the dwarf elder, is a deciduous shrub native to parts of North America, characterized by its compact growth habit and pubescent twigs. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Sambucus adnata var. puberula include its ability to boost the immune system, alleviate respiratory issues such as colds and flu, and exhibit antimicrobial properties against various pathogens. In herbal preparations, the plant's berries, leaves, and flowers can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
